// :auto-generated: true
// :source: docs/guides/validation.md
// Remove this header to take manual ownership of this file

= Validation Guide

Step-by-step tests for every piece of custom tooling in this
configuration. Work through each section in order — earlier sections
establish prerequisites for later ones. Mark steps `++[++x++]++` as you
go.

*Conventions used below:* - Commands prefixed `:` are typed in Neovim
command mode. - Commands prefixed `$` are run in a shell. -
`++<++leader++>++` = *Space*, `++<++localleader++>++` = *,* -
*Expected:* describes what a passing result looks like.

'''''

=== Prerequisites

Before starting, confirm the following are installed and available:

[source,sh]
----
which nvim glow python3 curl pandoc docker luac
----

* `nvim` — Neovim 0.11{plus}
* `glow` — Charmbracelet Markdown renderer (see
docs/guides/cli-console-mode.md for install)
* `python3` — required by PlantUML encoding
* `curl` — required by PlantUML and Confluence
* `pandoc` — required by MD→PDF and Confluence
* `docker` — required by all Docker-backed services
* `luac` — Lua compiler for syntax checking (optional but recommended)

'''''

=== 1. Lua Syntax Check

Verify all Lua files are syntactically valid.

[source,sh]
----
find ~/.config/nvim -name '*.lua' \
    -not -path '*/lazy/*' \
    -not -path '*/.serena/*' \
    -print0 | xargs -0 luac -p && echo "All OK"
----

* [x] 1.1 *Expected:* `All OK` — no output before it, exit code 0.

'''''

=== 2. Startup and Plugin Loading

* [x] 2.1 Open Neovim with no file argument:
+
[source,sh]
----
nvim
----
+
*Expected:* starts without any error or warning notifications.
* [ ] 2.2 Confirm lazy.nvim loaded all plugins without errors:
+
....
:Lazy
....
+
*Expected:* all plugins show `✓ loaded` or `○ not loaded` (lazy). None
show `✗ error`.
* [x] 2.3 Confirm leader keys:
+
....
:lua print(vim.g.mapleader, vim.g.maplocalleader)
....
+
*Expected:* `,` (space then comma).

'''''

=== 3. Console Detection

* [ ] 3.1 In a normal GUI/WSL session, confirm `is++_++console` is
false:
+
....
:lua print(require("config.terminal").is_console)
....
+
*Expected:* `false`
* [x] 3.2 In a headless/console session (no `$DISPLAY` or
`$WAYLAND++_++DISPLAY`):
+
[source,sh]
----
env -u DISPLAY -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY nvim
----
+
....
:lua print(require("config.terminal").is_console)
....
+
*Expected:* `true`
* [x] 3.3 Confirm WSL detection (WSL sessions only):
+
....
:lua print(require("config.terminal").is_wsl)
....
+
*Expected:* `true` on WSL, `false` otherwise.

'''''

=== 4. Markdown Preview — Glow (console)

*Precondition:* `glow` is on `$PATH`. Launch in console mode:

[source,sh]
----
env -u DISPLAY -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY nvim /tmp/test.md
----

Add content to the buffer:

....
# Test Heading

This is **bold** and _italic_ text.

- item one
- item two
....

* [x] 4.1 Confirm `glow.nvim` loaded and `markdown-preview.nvim` did
not:
+
....
:Lazy
....
+
*Expected:* `glow.nvim` shows loaded; `markdown-preview.nvim` shows not
loaded / skipped.
* [x] 4.2 Open preview via localleader:
+
....
,p
....
+
*Expected:* a *floating popup* with rounded border opens showing
rendered Markdown. No browser window launches.
* [x] 4.3 Close popup with `q` or `++<++Esc++>++`. *Expected:* popup
closes, cursor returns to source buffer.
* [ ] 4.4 *Orphaned popup recovery* — navigate away from the popup
without closing it: Open the popup with `,p`, then jump to another
window with `++<++C-w++>++w` until the glow buffer loses focus. Now
close it:
[arabic]
. Press `++<++C-w++>++w` until the glow buffer is active again.
. Press `q`. *Expected:* popup closes cleanly.
* [ ] 4.5 *Force-close all floats* — if step 4.4 fails, run the nuclear
option:
+
....
:lua for _, w in ipairs(vim.api.nvim_list_wins()) do if vim.api.nvim_win_get_config(w).relative ~= "" then vim.api.nvim_win_close(w, true) end end
....
+
*Expected:* all floating windows are closed. Main editing windows
remain.
* [ ] 4.6 Open via command directly:
+
....
:Glow
....
+
*Expected:* same floating popup behaviour.
* [ ] 4.7 Remove `glow` from PATH temporarily and confirm warning:
+
....
:lua vim.env.PATH = ""
,p
....
+
*Expected:* a `WARN` notification mentioning the install command. No
crash. Restore: `:lua vim.env.PATH = vim.fn.system("echo -n $PATH")`

'''''

=== 5. Markdown Preview — markdown-preview.nvim (GUI)

*Precondition:* running in a GUI session with `$DISPLAY` or
`$WAYLAND++_++DISPLAY` set.

[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.md
----

* [ ] 5.1 Confirm `markdown-preview.nvim` loaded and `glow.nvim` did
not:
+
....
:Lazy
....
+
*Expected:* `markdown-preview.nvim` loaded; `glow.nvim` not loaded.
* [ ] 5.2 Open browser preview:
+
....
,p
....
+
*Expected:* browser tab opens with live-rendered Markdown. No split
opens in Neovim.
* [ ] 5.3 Toggle off:
+
....
,p
....
+
*Expected:* browser tab closes (or preview server stops).

'''''

=== 6. Markdown Preview — Markserv (cross-page links)

*Precondition:* Docker is running.

[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/markserv/docker-compose.yml up -d
----

* [ ] 6.1 Verify the server started:
+
[source,sh]
----
curl -s http://localhost:8090/ | head -5
----
+
*Expected:* HTML content, no connection refused.
* [ ] 6.2 Open a markdown file and run:
+
....
,sp
....
+
*Expected:* an `INFO` notification (console) or browser tab (GUI) opens
at `http://localhost:8090/++<++file++>++`.
* [ ] 6.3 Stop the service when done:
+
[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/markserv/docker-compose.yml down
----

'''''

=== 7. PlantUML Docker Server

*Precondition:* Docker is running. This server is required for sections
8 and 9.

[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/plantuml-server/docker-compose.yml up -d
----

* [ ] 7.1 Verify the server responds:
+
[source,sh]
----
curl -s http://localhost:8080/txt/SoWkIImgAStDuNBAJrBGjLDmpCbCJbMmKiX8pSd9vt98pKi1IW80 | head -3
----
+
*Expected:* ASCII diagram output (lines with `-`, `++|++`, `++>++`).

Create a test PlantUML file:

[source,sh]
----
cat > /tmp/test.puml << 'EOF'
@startuml
Alice -> Bob: Hello
Bob --> Alice: Hi
@enduml
EOF
----

'''''

=== 8. PlantUML ASCII Preview

*Precondition:* PlantUML server from section 7 is running.

[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.puml
----

* [ ] 8.1 Run ASCII preview command:
+
....
:PumlPreviewAscii
....
+
*Expected:* a *floating popup* (rounded border, centered, ~70% of editor
size) opens containing the ASCII diagram.
* [ ] 8.2 Verify the scratch buffer properties:
+
....
:set buftype?
....
+
*Expected:* `buftype=nofile`
* [ ] 8.3 Close with `q`. *Expected:* popup closes, no "`save changes?`"
prompt.
* [ ] 8.4 Test via leader keymap:
+
....
<leader>pa
....
+
*Expected:* same floating popup.
* [ ] 8.5 Verify `:PumlPreview` routes to ASCII in console mode:
+
[source,sh]
----
env -u DISPLAY -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY nvim /tmp/test.puml
----
+
....
:PumlPreview
....
+
*Expected:* floating popup with ASCII art. No browser launch.
* [ ] 8.6 Verify `:PumlPreview` routes to PNG in GUI mode:
+
[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.puml
----
+
....
:PumlPreview
....
+
*Expected:* browser opens with PNG diagram. No Neovim split.

'''''

=== 9. Markdown → PDF Export (with diagrams)

*Precondition:* PlantUML server from section 7 is running.

Create a test file:

[source,sh]
----
cat > /tmp/test-diagrams.md << 'EOF'
# Test PDF

Some text.

```plantuml
@startuml
A -> B: test
@enduml
----

EOF

....

```sh
nvim /tmp/test-diagrams.md
....

* [ ] 9.1 Run export:
+
....
,dp
....
+
*Expected:* a notification appears; `/tmp/test-diagrams.pdf` is created
containing the diagram.
* [ ] 9.2 Verify the file exists:
+
[source,sh]
----
ls -la /tmp/test-diagrams.pdf
----
+
*Expected:* file exists with non-zero size.

'''''

=== 10. MARP Presentations

*Precondition:* Docker is running.

Create a test MARP file:

[source,sh]
----
cat > /tmp/test.md << 'EOF'
---
marp: true
---

# Slide 1

Content here.

---

# Slide 2

More content.
EOF
----

[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.md
----

* [ ] 10.1 Start MARP preview:
+
....
,mp
....
+
*Expected:* browser opens (GUI) or URL notification (console) at the
MARP preview server.
* [ ] 10.2 Export to HTML:
+
....
,mh
....
+
*Expected:* notification that `/tmp/test.html` was created.
* [ ] 10.3 Export to PDF:
+
....
,md
....
+
*Expected:* notification that `/tmp/test.pdf` was created.
* [ ] 10.4 Export to PPTX:
+
....
,mx
....
+
*Expected:* notification that `/tmp/test.pptx` was created.

'''''

=== 11. Avante AI — Ollama Backend

*Precondition:* Both steps are required on first use.

[source,sh]
----
# Step 1 — start the container
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/ollama/docker-compose.yml up -d

# Step 2 — pull the model (one-time, ~4 GB)
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/ollama/docker-compose.yml \
    exec ollama ollama pull llama3.1:8b
----

Verify ollama is reachable:

[source,sh]
----
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11434/ 
----

*Expected:* `Ollama is running`

* [ ] 11.1 Confirm keymaps are registered:
+
....
:lua print(vim.fn.maparg("<leader>aa","n") ~= "")
:lua print(vim.fn.maparg("<leader>ao","n") ~= "")
:lua print(vim.fn.maparg("<leader>ac","n") ~= "")
....
+
*Expected:* all three print `true`.
* [ ] 11.2 Open with `++<++leader++>++aa`. *Expected:* avante chat panel
opens, provider is `ollama`.
* [ ] 11.3 Type a question (`What is 2{plus}2?`) and submit
(`++<++CR++>++`). *Expected:* a response streams into the avante buffer.
No crash.
* [ ] 11.4 Test graceful failure — stop ollama then ask a question:
+
[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/ollama/docker-compose.yml stop
----
+
Press `++<++leader++>++ao`, type a question, submit. *Expected:* HTTP
error appears *inside* the avante buffer. Neovim remains fully
functional.
+
[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/ollama/docker-compose.yml start
----

'''''

=== 12. Avante AI — Copilot Backend

*Precondition:* Copilot is authenticated.

....
:Copilot status
....

*Expected:* `Copilot: Enabled`. If not, run `:Copilot setup`.

* [ ] 12.1 Switch to copilot provider and open: Press
`++<++leader++>++ac`. *Expected:* avante opens with provider `copilot`.
* [ ] 12.2 Ask a question and submit. *Expected:* Copilot response
streams in. No separate login prompt.

'''''

=== 13. Copilot Inline Completion

* [ ] 13.1 Open a code file (e.g. a `.lua` or `.py` file) and start
typing a function:
+
[source,lua]
----
-- In a .lua file
local function add(a, b)
----
+
*Expected:* a greyed-out inline suggestion appears.
* [ ] 13.2 Accept suggestion with `++<++Tab++>++`. *Expected:*
suggestion is inserted.
* [ ] 13.3 Test Copilot explain (visual mode): Select a few lines with
`V`, then press `++<++leader++>++gce`. *Expected:* Copilot provides an
explanation in a popup or panel.

'''''

=== 14. LSP

*Precondition:* at least one LSP server is configured (e.g. open a
`.lua` file — `lua++_++ls` if installed, or a `.fs` file for
`fsautocomplete`).

* [ ] 14.1 Open a Lua file with a known function:
+
[source,sh]
----
nvim ~/.config/nvim/lua/config/terminal.lua
----
* [ ] 14.2 Place cursor on a symbol and check hover:
+
....
K
....
+
*Expected:* a hover popup appears with type/doc info.
* [ ] 14.3 Go to definition:
+
....
gd
....
+
*Expected:* jumps to the definition site.
* [ ] 14.4 Check diagnostics panel:
+
....
:lua vim.diagnostic.open_float()
....
+
*Expected:* shows diagnostics for current line (or "`No diagnostics`" if
clean).

'''''

=== 16. Nvim-Tree File Explorer

* [ ] 16.1 Open file tree:

<leader>n
....
+

<C-f>

=== 17. Git (Fugitive {plus} Gitsigns)

*Precondition:* inside a git repository.

* [ ] 17.1 Open git status:
+
....
:Git
....
+
*Expected:* Fugitive status window opens.
* [ ] 17.2 Confirm gitsigns decorates changed lines. Modify a tracked
file and check the sign column. *Expected:* `{plus}` / `~` / `-` signs
appear in the gutter.
* [ ] 17.3 Preview a hunk:
+
....
:lua require("gitsigns").preview_hunk()
....
+
*Expected:* diff popup appears for the hunk under cursor.

'''''

=== 18. REST Client (Kulala)

*Precondition:* a `.http` file with a valid request.

[source,sh]
----
cat > /tmp/test.http << 'EOF'
GET https://httpbin.org/get

EOF
nvim /tmp/test.http
----

* [ ] 18.1 Run request under cursor:
+
....
,r
....
+
*Expected:* response pane opens with HTTP 200 and JSON body.
* [ ] 18.2 Replay last request:
+
....
,l
....
+
*Expected:* same response.
* [ ] 18.3 Open result pane manually:
+
....
,o
....
+
*Expected:* result pane opens/focuses.

'''''

=== 19. Common Lisp REPL (Conjure {plus} Swank)

*Precondition:* Docker is running.

[source,sh]
----
docker compose -f ~/.config/nvim/docker/sbcl-swank/docker-compose.yml up -d
# Wait a few seconds for Swank to start, then verify:
nc -z localhost 4005 && echo "Swank ready"
----

* [ ] 19.1 Open a `.lisp` file:
+
[source,sh]
----
echo '(+ 1 2)' > /tmp/test.lisp && nvim /tmp/test.lisp
----
* [ ] 19.2 Connect Conjure to Swank:
+
....
:ConjureConnect 4005
....
+
*Expected:* Conjure log shows connected to Swank.
* [ ] 19.3 Evaluate the expression under cursor:
+
....
,ee
....
+
*Expected:* Conjure log shows `=++>++ 3`.

'''''

=== 20. F++#++ REPL (iron.nvim)

*Precondition:* `dotnet` CLI is installed (`which dotnet`).

[source,sh]
----
echo 'printfn "Hello"' > /tmp/test.fsx && nvim /tmp/test.fsx
----

* [ ] 20.1 Start the REPL:
+
....
,rs
....
+
*Expected:* a `dotnet fsi` REPL opens in a split.
* [ ] 20.2 Send current line to REPL:
+
....
,l
....
+
*Expected:* REPL prints `Hello`.

'''''

=== 21. Haskell (haskell-tools {plus} GHCi)

----
echo 'main = putStrLn "hi"' > /tmp/Test.hs && nvim /tmp/Test.hs
----

+
....
:lua print(#vim.lsp.get_clients())
....
+
*Expected:* `1` or more.
* [ ] 21.2 Start GHCi REPL via haskell-tools:
+
....
,rr
....
+
*Expected:* GHCi REPL split opens.

'''''

=== 22. Confluence Integration

*Precondition:* `CONFLUENCE++_++EMAIL` and
`CONFLUENCE++_++API++_++TOKEN` env vars are set. See
`docs/guides/confluence.md` for setup.

[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.md
----

* [ ] 22.1 Confirm commands are registered:
+
....
:MdToConfluence
....
+
*Expected:* either prompts for a page ID / proceeds, or errors with a
meaningful message (not `E492: Not an editor command`).
* [ ] 22.2 Confirm pull command exists:
+
....
:MdFromConfluence
....
+
*Expected:* same — command exists and prompts/errors meaningfully.
* [ ] 22.3 Confirm comments command exists:
+
....
:MdConfluenceComments
....
+
*Expected:* command exists.

'''''

=== 23. Jira Integration

*Precondition:* `JIRA++_++EMAIL`, `JIRA++_++API++_++TOKEN`, and
`JIRA++_++BASE++_++URL` env vars are set. See `docs/guides/jira.md` for
setup.

[source,sh]
----
nvim /tmp/test.md
----

* [ ] 23.1 Confirm commands are registered:
+
....
:lua print(vim.fn.exists(":JiraCreateIssue"))
:lua print(vim.fn.exists(":JiraCreateStory"))
....
+
*Expected:* both print `2` (command exists).
* [ ] 23.2 Run create issue and confirm it prompts for a summary:
+
....
:JiraCreateIssue
....
+
*Expected:* an input prompt appears asking for a summary (or errors with
a meaningful message if credentials are missing).

'''''

* [ ] 24.1 Confirm OpenSpec commands are registered:
+
....
:lua print(vim.fn.exists(":OpenspecNew"))
:lua print(vim.fn.exists(":OpenspecStatus"))
:lua print(vim.fn.exists(":OpenspecList"))
....

*Expected:* all print `2`.

=== 25. Terminal Toggle

* [ ] 25.1 Open terminal split:
+
....
<leader>t
....
+
*Expected:* a terminal split opens at the bottom.
* [ ] 25.2 Exit terminal insert mode: Press `++<++Esc++>++` while in the
terminal. *Expected:* returns to normal mode (cursor visible in terminal
buffer) — does *not* close the terminal.
* [ ] 25.3 Toggle terminal closed:
+
....
<leader>t
....
+
*Expected:* terminal split closes.

'''''

=== 26. Formatting (conform.nvim)

* [ ] 26.1 Open a Lisp or F++#++ file with intentionally bad
indentation, then save:
+
....
:w
....
+
*Expected:* file is reformatted on save. No error notification.
* [ ] 26.2 Confirm format-on-save is active:
+
....
:lua print(require("conform").list_formatters()[1].name)
....
+
*Expected:* prints the active formatter name (e.g. `lsp++_++format`).

'''''

'''''

=== 27. System Clipboard

These tests verify the clipboard provider is correctly selected and that
the `++<++leader++>++` shortcuts work. Run in the environment that
matches the platform you want to verify.

==== 27.1 Identify the active provider

[source,vim]
----
:lua print(vim.g.clipboard and vim.g.clipboard.name or "unnamedplus (auto)")
----

[cols=",",options="header",]
|===
|Environment |Expected output
|WSL {plus} win32yank.exe |`win32yank-wsl`
|SSH / TTY / console |`OSC 52`
|GUI Linux |`unnamedplus (auto)`
|===

==== GUI Linux — xclip / xsel / wl-clipboard

* [ ] 27.2 Confirm `:checkhealth clipboard` shows a provider found
(xclip, xsel, or wl-copy):
+
[source,vim]
----
:checkhealth
----
+
Scroll to the *clipboard* section. *Expected:*
`OK: Clipboard tool found: xclip` (or xsel / wl-copy). No
`WARNING: No provider found`.
* [ ] 27.3 Yank a word to the system clipboard and paste it in a
separate application: Open a file, position cursor on a word, press
`++<++leader++>++y`. Switch to a text editor or browser address bar and
paste. *Expected:* the yanked word appears.
* [ ] 27.4 Cut a line with `++<++leader++>++d++<++leader++>++d` and
verify it lands in the system clipboard. *Expected:* pasting in an
external application shows the deleted line.
* [ ] 27.5 Copy something in an external application, switch to Neovim,
press `++<++leader++>++p`. *Expected:* the external text is pasted after
the cursor.

==== WSL — win32yank.exe

* [ ] 27.6 Verify `win32yank.exe` is on `$PATH`:
+
[source,sh]
----
which win32yank.exe
----
+
*Expected:* prints a `/mnt/c/...` path.
* [ ] 27.7 Confirm the provider name inside Neovim:
+
[source,vim]
----
:lua print(vim.g.clipboard.name)
----
+
*Expected:* `win32yank-wsl`
* [ ] 27.8 Yank a word with `++<++leader++>++y` and paste it in a
Windows application (e.g. Notepad). *Expected:* the word appears in
Notepad.
* [ ] 27.9 Copy text in Notepad, then paste in Neovim with
`++<++leader++>++p`. *Expected:* the Windows clipboard text is pasted.

==== SSH / Console — OSC 52

*Precondition:* connect via SSH from a terminal that supports OSC 52
(Windows Terminal, Alacritty, kitty, WezTerm, Ghostty). Or simulate
console mode:

[source,sh]
----
env -u DISPLAY -u WAYLAND_DISPLAY nvim /tmp/test.md
----

* [ ] 27.10 Confirm OSC 52 provider is active:
+
[source,vim]
----
:lua print(vim.g.clipboard.name)
----
+
*Expected:* `OSC 52`
* [ ] 27.11 Yank a word with `++<++leader++>++y`, then paste in a
*local* application on the machine running the terminal. *Expected:* the
word from the remote Neovim session appears locally.
* [ ] 27.12 Test `++<++leader++>++p` (paste from terminal clipboard).
*Expected:* either the local clipboard content is pasted, OR nothing is
pasted (terminals that block OSC 52 read for security). This is
acceptable — use `Shift{plus}Insert` or `Ctrl{plus}Shift{plus}V` as the
fallback.

==== Keymaps — all platforms

* [ ] 27.13 In visual mode, select text and press `++<++leader++>++y`.
*Expected:* selection is copied to the system clipboard without leaving
visual mode prematurely.
* [ ] 27.14 Press `++<++leader++>++Y` in normal mode. *Expected:* the
entire current line is copied to the system clipboard.
* [ ] 27.15 Press `++<++leader++>++P` in normal mode (paste before
cursor). *Expected:* clipboard content is pasted before the cursor
position.
* [ ] 27.16 In visual mode, press `++<++leader++>++p`. *Expected:*
pastes replacement text _without_ overwriting the clipboard (the
replaced text goes to the black-hole register, clipboard is preserved).

'''''

=== Summary Checklist

[cols=",,",options="header",]
|===
|Section |Feature |Tested
|1 |Lua syntax check |++[++ ++]++
|2 |Startup / plugin loading |++[++ ++]++
|3 |Console detection |++[++ ++]++
